tickets.title: vault-critic AG hang — network theory REFUTED by 6/04 probe; hang is INSIDE gemini's request path, not the network. The 6/04 03:30 run fired the IP-family probe AT the 120s-timeout moment: BOTH Google hosts (cloudcode-pa + play.googleapis) connected in ~15-26ms on IPv4 AND IPv6, and the api.openai.com control connected too — i.e. the network was fully up while gemini's authenticated model request hung to 120s and returned 0 tokens. The 6/03 play.googleapis.com connect-timeouts were a red herring (telemetry endpoint, intermittent; the actual model host cloudcode-pa was reachable). So eliminated now: MCP, OAuth-refresh, machine-asleep, AND raw network reachability. What remains is the authenticated Code Assist streaming call silently hanging (server-side throttle-as-hang on the preview model, or a CLI stream bug) — but note: curl-reachable ≠ authed-call-works, and the CLI swallows the real error, so further --debug captures won't reveal more. RECOMMENDATION: stop chasing the gemini hang — the circuit breaker now contains it ($0 impact) and the June-18 oauth EOL obsoletes the whole path. Collapse this into the migration decision below
tickets.headline: vault-critic AG hang — network theory REFUTED by 6/04 probe; hang is INSIDE gemini's request path, not the network. The 6/04 03:30 run fired the IP-family probe AT the 120s-timeout moment: BOTH Google hosts (cloudcode-pa + play.googleapis) connected in ~15-26ms on IPv4 AND IPv6, and the api.openai.com control connected too — i.e. the network was fully up while gemini's authenticated model request hung to 120s and returned 0 tokens. The 6/03 play.googleapis.com connect-timeouts were a red herring (telemetry endpoint, intermittent; the actual model host cloudcode-pa was reachable). So eliminated now: MCP, OAuth-refresh, machine-asleep, AND raw network reachability. What remains is the authenticated Code Assist streaming call silently hanging (server-side throttle-as-hang on the preview model, or a CLI stream bug) — but note: curl-reachable ≠ authed-call-works, and the CLI swallows the real error, so further --debug captures won't reveal more. RECOMMENDATION: stop chasing the gemini hang — the circuit breaker now contains it ($0 impact) and the June-18 oauth EOL obsoletes the whole path. Collapse this into the migration decision below

tickets.title: vault-critic AG — DECISION (6/04, Sean): Codex-only for now (`antigravity_enabled = false` shipped); no Gemini API spend. POST-JUNE-18 TASK: re-enable AG via the Antigravity CLI (uses the paid Google-One subscription, NOT the metered API) and A/B test whether the second opinion is worth keeping — flip `antigravity_enabled = true` (or run once with `--antigravity`), watch a few nightlies, compare expansion quality vs Codex-only + duration. If the Antigravity CLI hits the same silent hang, add a hard <120s stream timeout or drop AG for good. gemini-cli oauth-personal EOL is 6/18 (flag 45740200) — don't test the old gemini-cli path after that, go straight to Antigravity CLI
tickets.headline: vault-critic AG — DECISION (6/04, Sean): Codex-only for now (`antigravity_enabled = false` shipped); no Gemini API spend. If the Antigravity CLI hits the same silent hang, add a hard <120s stream timeout or drop AG for good. gemini-cli oauth-personal EOL is 6/18 (flag 45740200) — don't test the old gemini-cli path after that, go straight to Antigravity CLI

tickets.title: vault-critic budget waste — RESOLVED + VERIFIED on the 6/04 nightly. AG circuit-breaker tripped after 1 failure exactly as designed: manifest shows antigravity_calls=1, antigravity_skipped=7, codex_calls=8, articles=3 fresh + 5 backfill = 8 (was 5 on 6/03), codex_tokens=260K (was 145K), duration=323s (was 610s). Throughput +60%, budget headroom restored. With ~277s spare now, could raise backfill_max_per_run (currently 5) to drain the orphan backlog faster. Raise threshold to 2 (or 0=off) once AG is healthy/migrated

tickets.headline: Remove `VAULT_CRITIC_AG_DEBUG=1` from com.sean.agent.vault-critic.plist (and the run-once connectivity probe is gated behind it) once the 6/04 probe evidence pins the sub-cause — it appends gemini --debug noise + a ~10s probe to every nightly AG timeout

tickets.title: Obsidian-Git stale `.git/index.lock` recurs (fixed 5/31 and 6/03) — root cause is git commit crashing mid-write when Modern Standby cuts power during the 60-min auto-commit cycle. Permanent fix instead of repeated manual `rm .git/index.lock`: either widen the commit interval, gate commits on machine-awake, or add a pre-commit stale-lock sweep that only deletes a >5-min-old EMPTY index.lock with no live git process
tickets.headline: Obsidian-Git stale `.git/index.lock` recurs (fixed 5/31 and 6/03) — root cause is git commit crashing mid-write when Modern Standby cuts power during the 60-min auto-commit cycle. Permanent fix instead of repeated manual `rm .git/index.lock`: either widen the commit interval, gate commits on machine-awake, or add a pre-commit stale-lock sweep that only deletes a >5-min-old EMPTY index.lock with no live git process

tickets.title: ESCALATION (2026-06-03, B1b DINOv2 spike): this is BIGGER than "Flash-verified." sean-anchor's BODY turnarounds are proportion-OFF — head reads ~1:5.3 (profile-right), ~1:4.6 (back), ~1:4-4.5 (walk-cycle) instead of the 1:7 target — and were baked into the LOCKED Bible anyway because the Pass-2.5 similarity gate is record-only (it never measured proportion). So a re-verify on a pinned model isn't enough; the body plates need a proportion re-bake to clean 1:7, then re-lock. Evidence: anima PR #20 + evals/vision_critic/traces/dinov2-separation-2026-06-03.md.
